source.notes: User captured live response headers showing anthropic-ratelimit-unified-overage-status: rejected with overage-disabled-reason: org_level_disabled. Session at 0%, weekly at 11% — dashboard showed no exhaustion. Error surfaced as generic 'API Error: Rate limit reached' with no indication of the 5-hour rolling window or correct wait time.

hazard.summary: Rolling-window exhaustion with org-level overage disabled surfaces as identical error message to throughput throttle and quota exhaustion. Correct action is wait for 5-hour window reset — not 60s backoff and not weekly reset. Systems applying wrong fix loop indefinitely.
signals: Session usage: 0%, Weekly usage: 11% — dashboard shows no exhaustion
Error surfaced: API Error: Rate limit reached — identical to throughput throttle
Hard-blocks until 5-hour rolling window resets — regardless of dashboard state
Waiting 60s does not resolve — window reset follows anthropic-ratelimit-unified-5h-reset timestamp

constraints: Rolling-window exhaustion with overage disabled must be classified as STOP — not WAIT
STOP classification must distinguish window-reset clock from weekly-reset clock
Error message must surface which limiter fired — not collapse all 429s into one message
anthropic-ratelimit-unified-overage-status header must be consulted before applying retry logic
Dashboard-visible usage metrics are insufficient to determine limit state — response headers are required
knobs: anthropic-ratelimit-unified-5h-reset — unix timestamp for window reset
anthropic-ratelimit-unified-7d-reset — unix timestamp for weekly reset
verification: anthropic-ratelimit-unified-overage-status: rejected → classified as STOP, not WAIT
Error message surfaces: 'Rolling window exhausted. Resets in ~Xh' — not generic 'rate limit reached'
60s backoff not applied — system waits for window reset
Dashboard state not used as sole indicator of limit exhaustion
Three distinct cases produce three distinct error messages and actions
notes: This receipt documents a third distinct 429 sub-class not previously captured in the corpus. The three cases now documented: (1) throughput throttle — WAIT 60s + retry, (2) quota exhaustion — STOP until weekly reset, (3) rolling-window + overage disabled — STOP until 5-hour window resets. All three currently surface as 'API Error: Rate limit reached' in Claude Code CLI. The anthropic-ratelimit-unified-overage-status header is the classification signal for case 3. Primary evidence: live response headers captured by affected user, session 0% / weekly 11% at time of failure. The dashboard does not expose 5-hour window utilization, making this failure class invisible to users. Full header schema confirmed via post-resolution capture: anthropic-ratelimit-unified-* family exposes 5h and 7d window state, utilization float, reset timestamps, representative-claim (five_hour|seven_day), fallback-percentage, and overage-status. Smoking gun combination: unified-5h-utilization near 1.0 + unified-status: blocked + overage-status: rejected. The fallback-percentage field (0.5) indicates fallback headroom exists but is blocked by org_level_disabled policy.
